formulas.name: Stack Operations
formulas.formula: Push: Add an element to the top of the stack. Pop: Remove an element from the top of the stack. Peek: Get the top element from the stack without removing it.
formulas.notes: These operations are the fundamental operations in a stack data structure.

formulas.name: Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) Principle
formulas.formula: Elements are added and removed from the stack in the reverse order of their addition.
formulas.notes: This principle is the defining characteristic of a stack data structure.

formulas.name: Stack Implementation
formulas.formula: Stack can be implemented using arrays or linked lists.
formulas.notes: Arrays are often used for static stacks, while linked lists are used for dynamic stacks.

formulas.name: Empty Stack
formulas.formula: A stack is empty if it contains no elements.
formulas.notes: This is the initial state of a stack when it is created.

formulas.name: Full Stack
formulas.formula: A stack is full if it contains the maximum number of elements it can hold.
formulas.notes: This state occurs when a stack has reached its capacity.

formulas.name: Stack Overflow
formulas.formula: A stack overflow occurs when a new element is added to a full stack.
formulas.notes: This is an error condition that must be handled in a stack implementation.

formulas.name: Stack Underflow
formulas.formula: A stack underflow occurs when an element is removed from an empty stack.
formulas.notes: This is an error condition that must be handled in a stack implementation.
